callReview.totalNote: total of 222 calls from Jan 1 to Oct 31

calls.summary: Melissa Grant called to ask how much Contoura Vision LASIK costs and whether she was a candidate. Agent Oscar explained the roughly $2,500-per-eye price, walked her through $0-down financing options, and booked a free LASIK consultation at the Dallas office. Melissa left excited and pre-registered for the patient portal.

Harold Jennings described cloudy, worsening vision and asked about cataract surgery and premium lens choices. Agent Lila reviewed monofocal versus multifocal and toric IOL options and confirmed Medicare coverage, then scheduled a cataract surgery consultation in Fort Worth. Harold was reassured and confirmed the time.

Priya Nair asked about EVO ICL versus LASIK for her high prescription. Agent Rosey explained the removable Collamer lens and why ICL might suit her thin corneas, then booked a comprehensive eye exam to start the workup. Priya thanked the agent for the clear explanation.

New patient Diego Ramirez called to schedule his first visit after moving to the Dallas area. Agent Mary collected his name, date of birth, and insurance information, verified coverage, and discussed financing for any elective work, then booked a comprehensive eye exam and initial consultation. Diego confirmed his appointment and portal signup.

Sandra Willis called about chronic burning, watery eyes and seasonal allergy flare-ups. Agent Lee explained the dry eye evaluation and possible punctal plug and drop therapies, then scheduled an appointment at the Mesquite location. Sandra was satisfied with the plan.

Karen Alvarez called questioning a bill after her cataract surgery, saying the premium lens upgrade charge was higher than quoted. Agent Sue pulled the account, explained the out-of-pocket portion above Medicare, and opened a billing review. Karen accepted the explanation but wanted a follow-up call.

Robert Chen called to check the status of his new glasses order placed at the optical shop two weeks ago. Agent Mike looked up the lab order and found it was still in fabrication, then gave an updated pickup date. Robert was mildly frustrated by the delay.

Existing patient Gloria Estrada called to reschedule her upcoming cataract surgery due to a family conflict. Agent Jennifer located the surgery center booking and moved it to a later date, then updated the pre-op instructions. Gloria was relieved but concerned about the wait.

Thomas Boyd called because a lens had popped out of the frames he bought last month and wanted to know about the warranty. Agent Rose explained the optical warranty terms and asked him to bring the glasses to the nearest location for a repair. Thomas was annoyed the frames failed so soon.

Angela Ruiz disputed how her insurance was applied to her recent eye exam, saying she was billed for a service she thought was covered. Agent Oscar reviewed the claim and explained the difference between a routine and medical visit. Angela remained unconvinced and requested escalation.

Frank DeLuca called to complain about a long wait at the Arlington office during his glaucoma follow-up. Agent Lila apologized, documented the feedback, and offered to note his file for priority scheduling. Frank stayed irritated about the lost time.

Nancy Holt called to cancel her upcoming appointment and said she'd found another provider closer to home. Agent Lee tried to retain her by offering an earlier slot but she declined and the appointment was cancelled. The call ended on a cool note.

Walter Simmons called after hours about his post-op eye drops and the call rolled to voicemail. No agent was available and he left a message asking for a callback about redness and discomfort. The message flagged an urgent tone but went unanswered on this call.

Denise Park called upset about being sent to collections over an unpaid balance she claimed she never received. Agent Sue searched the account, found a bounced statement email, and tried to set up a payment plan. Denise demanded a supervisor and stayed hostile.

Victor Malone called to complain that his blepharoplasty results looked uneven and he wanted someone to explain the outcome. Agent Mike listened, apologized, and offered to schedule a post-op review with the surgeon. Victor refused any further visit and ended the call frustrated.

unmetSignals: Caller concerns about cost not addressed

prompts.question: Is the customer trying to solve a specific problem?
prompts.answer: Yes. The caller is a 42-year-old new patient interested in LASIK vision correction to reduce dependence on glasses for distance vision, with a desired timeline of 2–3 months for the procedure.

prompts.question: What are the customer's expectations?
prompts.answer: The caller expects a free consultation to determine LASIK candidacy, wants pricing information, and anticipates a relatively quick path from consultation to surgery, but these expectations were not explicitly set or confirmed during the call.

prompts.question: How could the agent handle the call better?
prompts.answer: Marcus should have captured the email address (attempted but not completed) and set clear expectations about what to expect during the consultation visit—pre-exam testing, surgeon consultation, financing review, and typical timeline—to reduce patient anxiety and no-shows.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good afternoon, thank you for calling Key-Whitman Eye Center. This is Marcus speaking. How can I help you today?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi Marcus, yes, I'm interested in learning more about LASIK. A friend of mine had it done at your Dallas location and loves it, so I wanted to explore options for myself.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's wonderful. We're so glad to hear your friend had a great experience. I'd be happy to help you schedule a free consultation with one of our surgeons. May I have your full name, please?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Of course. It's Sarah Mitchell.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect, Sarah. And just to confirm—you're interested in LASIK vision correction to reduce your dependence on glasses, is that correct?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Exactly. I wear contacts and glasses depending on the situation, and I'd love to have the freedom to not need either of those. That's why I'm looking into LASIK.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I completely understand. That's what we call 'Freedom From Glasses,' and it's been our mission for over sixty years. To get you started, what ZIP code are you located in?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I'm in the 75201 area, so I'm pretty central in Dallas.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Excellent. We have a wonderful location right there that serves central Dallas. Now, to get you into our system, I'll need your phone number.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you. And Sarah, may I also get your email address so we can send you pre-consultation information and any financing options we have available?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Um, I'd prefer not to share that right now. Can you just call me to confirm the appointment?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Absolutely, no problem. We can definitely reach you by phone. Now, when would be a good time for you to come in for your consultation? We have morning and afternoon slots available.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: How about sometime next week? I'm pretty flexible. Maybe a Tuesday or Wednesday morning?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. Let me check our schedule. How about Tuesday, June 13th at 9:30 AM at our Dallas location?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: That works great for me.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Wonderful. So Sarah, I have you down for Tuesday, June 13th at 9:30 AM for your LASIK consultation at our Dallas clinic. Is there anything else I can help clarify before we wrap up?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: No, I think that's it. I'm excited to learn more about my options and pricing.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great! Just so you know, our surgeon will go over everything during your visit, including candidacy testing and pricing for your specific prescription. We'll see you on June 13th. Thank you for choosing Key-Whitman Eye Center, Sarah. Have a wonderful day!

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Thank you, Marcus. See you next week!

callDetail.aiSummary: Sarah Mitchell, a new patient in the Dallas 75201 ZIP code, called to schedule a LASIK consultation after hearing positive feedback from a friend. Marcus successfully qualified her vision-correction goal, confirmed her service area, captured her phone number, and booked her for a consultation on Tuesday, June 13th at 9:30 AM. However, the email address was not captured, and patient expectations regarding the consultation process, pre-exam procedures, and timeline were not clearly set, which could impact engagement and show-rate.

comment.author: Kyle Paklaian
comment.text: @Marcus, great structure and professional tone on this intake call. You nailed the greeting, name capture, and appointment scheduling. Two quick coaching notes: (1) When Sarah hesitated on the email, don't drop it entirely—offer an alternative like SMS or a follow-up text instead, so we have a secondary contact; (2) Before hanging up, walk her through what to expect at the visit (testing time, surgeon consultation, financing review)—it reduces no-shows and sets healthy expectations. Nice job overall!

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Key-Whitman Eye Center, this is Marisol. How can I help you today?
highlights: Thank you for calling Key-Whitman Eye Center

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi, I'm interested in getting LASIK. I've worn glasses forever and I wanted to see about getting rid of them.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's wonderful, I'd be happy to help you explore your options for freedom from glasses. Have you been a patient with us before, or is this your first time?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: This would be my first time. I've never been in.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect, welcome! Let me get you set up as a new patient. May I have your full name, please?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Sure, it's Daniel Ortega.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you, Daniel. And what's the best phone number and email to reach you?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: My cell is 469-555-0182, and my email is danielortega82@gmail.com.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Got it. Do you happen to know your prescription, or if you have astigmatism? That helps us know which procedure might fit best.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I'm pretty nearsighted and I think I have a little astigmatism. My eyes are otherwise healthy.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great. Based on that, our doctors often recommend Contoura Vision, which is a topography-guided LASIK that maps thousands of points on the cornea for a very customized correction. That runs about $2,500 per eye.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Okay, that sounds interesting. Is there anything else I should consider?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: If your prescription is on the higher side, the surgeon may also discuss EVO ICL, an implantable lens option, but the best way to know is a free LASIK consultation where we measure your eyes and confirm candidacy.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: A free consultation sounds perfect. How do I set that up?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can schedule that for you right now. Which is more convenient — our Dallas location or Fort Worth?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Dallas works best for me.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Wonderful. I have an opening this Thursday, August 14th at 10 AM at our Dallas office. Does that work for you?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, Thursday at 10 works great.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're all set, Daniel — your free LASIK consultation is booked for Thursday at 10 AM in Dallas. Please avoid wearing contacts a few days before, and plan for about 90 minutes. I'll send a confirmation to your email.
highlights: free LASIK consultation is booked for Thursday at 10 AM

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Perfect, thank you so much for your help.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: My pleasure, Daniel. We look forward to seeing you Thursday. Thank you for calling Key-Whitman Eye Center, and have a great day!
highlights: Thank you for calling Key-Whitman Eye Center

aiSummary.summary: New patient Daniel Ortega called Key-Whitman Eye Center interested in LASIK to reduce his dependence on glasses, noting he is nearsighted with mild astigmatism. The agent, Marisol, recommended Contoura Vision topography-guided LASIK (~$2,500 per eye) and mentioned EVO ICL as a possible alternative for higher prescriptions. She captured his contact details and scheduled a free LASIK consultation at the Dallas location. The call closed politely with a confirmation email promised.
keyPoints: Caller seeking LASIK for freedom from glasses; nearsighted with astigmatism
Recommended Contoura Vision LASIK and mentioned EVO ICL option
Free LASIK consultation booked at Dallas location
Name, cell number, and email captured for follow-up
aiSummary.outcome: Consultation booked — Thursday, Aug 14 at 10 AM, Dallas

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hello! Thanks for reaching out to Key-Whitman Eye Center. I'm here to help you explore our vision correction options. Are you interested in learning more about LASIK, cataract surgery, or another eye care service today?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Hi, yes. I've been thinking about LASIK for a while now. I'm tired of wearing glasses all the time.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great! LASIK is an excellent choice for freedom from glasses. To help me recommend the right procedure for you, have you had any previous eye surgery or do you have any eye conditions we should know about?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: No, I've never had eye surgery. My eyes are pretty healthy, just myopic and a little astigmatism.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. Myopia and astigmatism are ideal for LASIK correction. We offer several options: Standard LASIK around $2,200 per eye, Contoura Vision at $2,500 per eye for extra precision, or Wavelight Plus at $2,600 per eye with ray-tracing technology. Do any of those options sound interesting to you?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: What's the difference between them? I want the best outcome.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Excellent question. Standard LASIK uses laser technology to reshape your cornea. Contoura maps 22,000 points on your cornea for a highly customized correction. Wavelight Plus uses whole-eye ray-tracing for the most personalized treatment. Many patients choose Wavelight Plus for the best visual clarity. Would you like to schedule a free consultation so Dr. Whitman's team can evaluate which is best for your eyes?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Yes, I'd like to do that. How soon can I come in?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Wonderful! We have several convenient locations across the Dallas-Fort Worth area. Are you available this Saturday, March 15th at 9:00 AM or would a weekday work better for you?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Saturday morning works great for me. March 15th at 9 AM is perfect.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Excellent! I've scheduled your LASIK consultation for Saturday, March 15th at 9:00 AM. Please bring your insurance card and photo ID. Is 972-555-0147 the best number to reach you with a reminder?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Yes, that's my cell number. Thanks!

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect! We'll send you a reminder on Friday. If you have any questions before your appointment, just call us at 214-555-EYES. Looking forward to helping you achieve freedom from glasses!

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Thank you! See you Saturday.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hi there, thanks for calling Key-Whitman Eye Center. This is Sarah. Are you calling about LASIK, cataract surgery, or another eye care service today?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Yeah, hi. I've been thinking about LASIK for a while now. I'm tired of wearing glasses all the time.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's great! A lot of our patients tell us the same thing. Have you had any prior eye surgery or do you have any eye conditions like dry eye or glaucoma that we should know about?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: No, nothing like that. My eyes are pretty healthy. I just have myopia and a little bit of astigmatism.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. That's definitely something we can help with. We have several LASIK options, from our standard laser to advanced procedures like Contoura Vision and Wavelight Plus. Have you looked into the difference between those, or would you like us to go over it?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: I've done a little research, but honestly, I'm not totally sure what the differences are. I want to know what the cost is going to be.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Absolutely. Standard LASIK typically runs about twenty-two hundred dollars per eye. Our Contoura Vision, which maps your cornea in detail for a more customized correction, is around twenty-five hundred per eye. And Wavelight Plus, which uses ray-tracing technology, is about twenty-six hundred per eye.

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Okay, so maybe around forty-four hundred for both eyes with the standard option?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yes, that's right. And just so you know, we do offer financing options with zero down if that helps. The first step is to come in for a free consultation and evaluation. That way, our doctors can determine which procedure is the best fit for your eyes. Does that sound good?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Yeah, that makes sense. When could I come in?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great! We have availability at our Dallas location on Friday, March fifteenth at ten in the morning, or we also have Thursday, March fourteenth at two in the afternoon. Which works better for you?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Friday the fifteenth at ten a.m. sounds perfect.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Excellent. I've got you down for Friday, March fifteenth at ten a.m. at our Dallas clinic. Just a quick reminder: please bring your insurance card and any glasses or contacts prescription you have. We'll call you at this number the day before to confirm. Is this the best callback number for you?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Yeah, that's the number. Thanks!

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're welcome! We're excited to see you. If you have any questions before your appointment, feel free to give us a call back at eight seven seven, nine three six, twenty-nine thirty-three. Have a great day!

brandConversationRules: Intro + offer: Hi, I'm Key-Whitman Eye Center's AI assistant, here to help you get a personalized vision correction quote and find your best path to freedom from glasses—we're currently offering $0-down special financing on LASIK; would you like to get started?

Qualify one question at a time: I'll ask a few quick questions one at a time—which service you're interested in (LASIK, EVO ICL, cataract surgery, or an eye exam), your current prescription strength or main symptoms, whether you wear glasses or contacts and for how long, your ideal timeline for treatment, and your ZIP code so I can confirm which of our 9 DFW clinics is closest to you.

Estimate then book: Once I confirm we serve your ZIP in the Dallas–Fort Worth area, I'll share a preliminary price range based on what you've told me, recommend a consultation with one of our ophthalmologists for an exact quote and candidacy evaluation, propose a specific day and time at your nearest clinic, and let you know you'll receive a reminder text before your appointment with a number to call if anything changes.

qaPairs.question: How much does LASIK cost at Key-Whitman?
qaPairs.answer: LASIK pricing varies by the technology and your specific prescription, so the best way to get an accurate figure is a free consultation where we evaluate your eyes—would you like me to book one at your nearest clinic?

qaPairs.question: Do you offer financing for LASIK?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, we offer special $0-down financing options to make vision correction affordable; I can go over the details during a consultation—shall I schedule one for you?

qaPairs.question: Am I a candidate for LASIK?
qaPairs.answer: Candidacy depends on your prescription, corneal thickness, and eye health, which we assess during a complimentary evaluation—let's book your consultation so a specialist can confirm.

qaPairs.question: What's the difference between LASIK and EVO ICL?
qaPairs.answer: LASIK reshapes the cornea while EVO ICL implants a removable lens ideal for higher prescriptions, and the right choice depends on your eyes—our doctors can recommend the best option at a consultation I can set up for you.

qaPairs.question: Does Medicare cover cataract surgery?
qaPairs.answer: Standard cataract surgery is typically covered by Medicare and most insurance, with premium lens upgrades available at additional cost; we'll review your specific coverage during your consultation—want me to schedule it?

qaPairs.question: What are my lens options for cataract surgery?
qaPairs.answer: We offer monofocal, multifocal, and toric premium IOLs that can reduce your dependence on glasses, and your surgeon will help you choose during an evaluation—let's get you booked.

qaPairs.question: How long does LASIK recovery take?
qaPairs.answer: Most patients notice improved vision within a day or two and resume normal activities quickly, though your surgeon will give personalized guidance at your consultation—shall I schedule one?

qaPairs.question: Do you treat glaucoma?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, we provide comprehensive glaucoma diagnosis, medication management, and laser treatment; I'd recommend booking an evaluation with one of our specialists—want me to find a time?

qaPairs.question: Can you help with dry eye?
qaPairs.answer: We offer diagnostic evaluations and a range of dry eye and allergy treatments tailored to you; the first step is a consultation—would you like me to set that up?

qaPairs.question: Where are your locations?
qaPairs.answer: We have 9 clinics across the Dallas–Fort Worth Metroplex; if you share your ZIP code I can tell you which one is closest and book your visit there.

qaPairs.question: Do you have Spanish-speaking staff?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, we have Spanish-speaking team members and doctors to assist you; I can schedule your appointment and note your language preference—shall we get started?

qaPairs.question: Do you offer eyelid surgery?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, our oculoplastic specialists perform blepharoplasty and other eyelid procedures for both function and appearance; I'd be happy to book a consultation to discuss your goals.

qaPairs.question: Can I get a comprehensive eye exam?
qaPairs.answer: Absolutely, we provide full vision and eye-health exams at all our locations, often covered by insurance—share your ZIP and I'll book you at the nearest clinic.

qaPairs.question: How do I schedule an appointment?
qaPairs.answer: I can book it for you right now—just let me know the service you're interested in and your ZIP code, and I'll find the best time at your closest DFW clinic.

qaPairs.question: What is Contoura Vision LASIK?
qaPairs.answer: Contoura Vision is a topography-guided LASIK that maps thousands of points on your cornea for a highly customized correction; our doctors can determine if it's right for you at a consultation.

qaPairs.question: Do you accept my insurance?
qaPairs.answer: We work with many major insurance plans; if you share your provider we can verify coverage, and I'd be glad to book your appointment to review the details.

qaPairs.question: What is PRK and how is it different from LASIK?
qaPairs.answer: PRK is a flapless surface procedure often suited to thinner corneas or active lifestyles, while LASIK creates a corneal flap; a consultation will determine your best fit—want me to schedule one?

qaPairs.question: How long has Key-Whitman been in business?
qaPairs.answer: We've been a leader in DFW eye care since 1961, led by Dr. Jeffrey Whitman; I'd love to book you a consultation to experience our care firsthand.

qaPairs.question: Can I do a self-evaluation before booking?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, we offer online LASIK and cataract self-evaluation quizzes, but a consultation gives you a definitive answer—I can set one up whenever you're ready.

qaPairs.question: Do you offer cosmetic procedures like Botox?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, our oculoplastic team offers Botox and dermal fillers around the eyes and brow; I can book a consultation to discuss your options and pricing.

smsScreenpop.greeting: Hi James! This is Sarah from Key-Whitman Eye Center. We're all set for your cataract consultation this Friday at 2:00 PM at our Fort Worth location. Our specialist will discuss your lens options, including our premium multifocal implants. Feel free to text back with any questions — we're here to help!

convo.text: Hi there! Thanks for calling Key-Whitman Eye Center. I'm the AI assistant helping us route you quickly today. Are you calling about a vision correction procedure like LASIK or cataract surgery, or do you need something else?
Yeah, hi. I've been looking into LASIK. I saw your Wavelight Plus option online and wanted to know more about the cost and whether I'd be a good candidate.

Perfect! LASIK is a great option for the right candidate. Wavelight Plus with our ray-tracing technology runs around $2,600 per eye. To see if you're eligible, we'd want to do a quick in-person self-evaluation and eye exam. Have you had an eye exam in the last year, or would this be your first time seeing us?
No, I've never been to your clinic before. I'm pretty serious about this—I just want to get rid of my glasses. When could I come in for that evaluation?

Excellent! I'm routing you now to our New Patient Consultation team—they'll get you booked for a comprehensive eye exam and LASIK self-evaluation with one of our doctors. You're in great hands. One moment, please.

voiceRoutingDemo.routedSubtitle: AI Agent qualified caller intent and matched to the right team
